verdict.summary: Redis Cluster redirecting commands to the correct node. Client sent command to a node that does not own the target hash slot.

dead_ends.action: Retry the command on the same node hoping the slot migrates back
dead_ends.why_fails: MOVED is permanent; the slot belongs to a different node. Retrying on the same node always fails.

dead_ends.action: Connect to a single cluster node and ignore redirects
dead_ends.why_fails: Single-node connection misses all keys not in that node's slots; most operations fail

workarounds.how: redis-py: RedisCluster(host='...', port=7000); Jedis: JedisCluster; ioredis: new Cluster([{host, port}])

workarounds.action: Handle MOVED by updating the slot-to-node mapping in the client
workarounds.how: Cluster-aware clients do this automatically; raw clients must parse MOVED and reconnect to the target node

workarounds.action: Handle ASK by sending ASKING command followed by the original command to the target node
workarounds.how: ASK means slot is migrating; send ASKING + command to target node (temporary redirect, unlike MOVED)
